page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized, high-performance virtual server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processor operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system provides 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with hyperthreading enabled, supported by 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. The processor includes a 60 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance profiles across memory and compute tasks. The instance achieves top-tier results in single-core Geekbench testing, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and image and text processing. Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks place the instance in the top 25%, reflecting the efficiency of its 5 GHz DDR5 memory subsystem. Additionally, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in static web serving throughput, Passmark CPU encryption, and low-latency LLM inference for small and medium-sized models. Multi-core performance and database operations fall within the average range.

The r7iz.8xlarge does not include local NVMe storage, requiring external network-attached storage solutions. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high-density memory configuration paired with high-frequency processing cores, making it a viable option for workloads requiring rapid memory access without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, real-time analytics, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6455B processor with 16 physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and 256.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, single-threaded decompression, and image processing, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and provides a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Qualitatively, it delivers high resource density for memory-bound applications without requiring GPU acceleration. Its high clock speed and DDR5 memory architecture provide a balanced tradeoff between raw compute power and memory throughput. The r7iz.8xlarge is suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 32 vCPUs, and 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier single-core and memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual server featuring DDR5 RAM and advanced Intel AMX instructions for demanding database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processor with 32 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz
256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core performance and single-threaded decompression benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for relational databases, memory-intensive applications, and CPU-based inference
